candidates.title: SubgraphService value conservation across collect/withdraw paths
candidates.paid_impact_path: unauthorized or incorrect movement of funds
candidates.invariant: Tracked balances plus paid/withdrawn amounts should never exceed deposits or escrowed funds.
candidates.local_test_goal: Model deposits, collection, cancellation, and withdrawal-like actions, then assert conservation after every sequence.
candidates.rejection_rule: Reject if the real contract already enforces conservation or the scenario cannot map to an accepted paid impact.

candidates.title: SubgraphService authorization/state-transition invariant
candidates.paid_impact_path: unauthorized action that changes payment, allocation, or agreement state
candidates.invariant: Only the intended actor should move an agreement, allocation, payment destination, or claim state into a value-affecting status.
candidates.local_test_goal: Fuzz caller roles and lifecycle orderings, then assert unauthorized callers cannot produce value-affecting state changes.
candidates.rejection_rule: Reject if unauthorized calls revert or only produce non-paid informational effects.

candidates.title: SubgraphService bounded-rate and overflow invariant
candidates.paid_impact_path: payer cap bypass, locked funds, or blocked fee collection
candidates.invariant: Inputs accepted by SubgraphService should not overflow before downstream caps, slippage checks, or collection limits apply.
candidates.local_test_goal: Use boundary values around rate, entity count, elapsed time, and slippage for external/thegraph-contracts/packages/subgraph-service/contracts/SubgraphService.sol, then assert collection is capped, recoverable, or safely reverted.
candidates.rejection_rule: Reject if overflow requires unrealistic self-harm terms and a safer collection path always recovers funds.
